default.description: Balanced evaluation - equal weight to length, range, and other factors
default.use_case: General purpose evaluation balancing all difficulty aspects

speed-focused.description: Emphasizes tempo and speed - fast songs are much harder
speed-focused.use_case: When you want rapid-fire, fast-tempo songs to rank as harder
speed-focused.command: python3 evaluate_difficulty.py --weights length:15 range:15 actions:15 avg_dur:35 stdev:15 min_dur:5

range-focused.description: Emphasizes wide note ranges - wide ranges are much harder
range-focused.use_case: When vocal range is the primary difficulty factor
range-focused.command: python3 evaluate_difficulty.py --weights length:20 range:40 actions:15 avg_dur:15 stdev:5 min_dur:5

endurance-focused.description: Emphasizes song length and note count - long songs are much harder
endurance-focused.use_case: When stamina and endurance are the primary difficulty factors
endurance-focused.command: python3 evaluate_difficulty.py --weights length:40 range:15 actions:30 avg_dur:10 stdev:3 min_dur:2

technical-focused.description: Emphasizes technical complexity - speed, range, and variability matter most
technical-focused.use_case: For technically challenging pieces with complex patterns
technical-focused.command: python3 evaluate_difficulty.py --weights length:10 range:30 actions:10 avg_dur:25 stdev:20 min_dur:5

beginner-friendly.description: Simplified evaluation - only length and range matter significantly
beginner-friendly.use_case: For beginner programs where only melody range and duration matter
beginner-friendly.command: python3 evaluate_difficulty.py --weights length:30 range:40 actions:10 avg_dur:10 stdev:5 min_dur:5

precision-focused.description: Emphasizes minimum note duration - very short notes are much harder
precision-focused.use_case: When precision and control are critical
precision-focused.command: python3 evaluate_difficulty.py --weights length:15 range:20 actions:15 avg_dur:20 stdev:15 min_dur:15

action-focused.description: Emphasizes note count - more notes means more difficult
action-focused.use_case: When complexity of sequences matters most
action-focused.command: python3 evaluate_difficulty.py --weights length:20 range:20 actions:40 avg_dur:10 stdev:5 min_dur:5

balanced-technical.description: Balanced but slightly emphasizing technical aspects (speed + variability)
balanced-technical.use_case: General purpose with slight emphasis on technical difficulty
balanced-technical.command: python3 evaluate_difficulty.py --weights length:20 range:25 actions:15 avg_dur:20 stdev:12 min_dur:8

smooth-only.description: Ignores rapid notes - emphasizes only sustained, slow pieces
smooth-only.command: python3 evaluate_difficulty.py --weights length:30 range:30 actions:20 avg_dur:10 stdev:5 min_dur:5

extreme-speed.description: Extreme - almost all weight on speed metrics
extreme-speed.command: python3 evaluate_difficulty.py --weights length:5 range:5 actions:5 avg_dur:50 stdev:20 min_dur:10

extreme-range.description: Extreme - almost all weight on note range
extreme-range.command: python3 evaluate_difficulty.py --weights length:5 range:70 actions:5 avg_dur:10 stdev:5 min_dur:5

equal-all.use_case: When all factors should be considered equally
equal-all.command: python3 evaluate_difficulty.py --weights length:16 range:17 actions:17 avg_dur:17 stdev:17 min_dur:16

complexity-emphasis.use_case: When complex note patterns are harder than simple ones
complexity-emphasis.command: python3 evaluate_difficulty.py --weights length:15 range:20 actions:25 avg_dur:15 stdev:15 min_dur:10

usage_notes.note_1: Each preset has a 'command' field showing how to use it
usage_notes.note_2: All weights must sum to 100 (script validates this)
usage_notes.note_3: Run with: python3 evaluate_difficulty.py --weights KEY1:VAL1 KEY2:VAL2 ...
usage_notes.note_4: Valid keys: length, range, actions, avg_dur, stdev, min_dur
usage_notes.note_5: Preview changes before updating: python3 evaluate_difficulty.py --output changes
usage_notes.note_6: Update index after reviewing: python3 evaluate_difficulty.py --update-index
